testcase.name: test_get_edition_by_isbn

failure.message: AssertionError: Calls not found. Expected: [call('https://openlibrary.org/api/books.json?bibkeys=ISBN:0374202915'), call().raise_for_status(), call().json(), call('https://openlibrary.org/books/OL23575801M.json'), call().raise_for_status(), call().json()] Actual: [call('https://openlibrary.org/api/books.json?bibkeys=ISBN:0374202915'), call().json(), call('https://openlibrary.org/books/OL23575801M.json'), call().json()]

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_openlibrary.TestOpenLibrary testMethod=test_get_edition_by_isbn> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4357540368'>
@patch('treq.get') def test_get_edition_by_isbn(self, mock_get): isbn_lookup_response = { 'ISBN:0374202915': { 'info_url': 'https://openlibrary.org/books/OL23575801M/Marie_LaVeau' } } edition_response = {'key': "/books/OL23575801M", 'title': 'test'} mock_get.return_value.json.side_effect = [ isbn_lookup_response, edition_response, ] book = self.ol.Edition.get(isbn='0374202915') > mock_get.assert_has_calls( [ call(f"{self.ol.base_url}/api/books.json?bibkeys=ISBN:0374202915"), call().raise_for_status(), call().json(), call(f"{self.ol.base_url}/books/OL23575801M.json"), call().raise_for_status(), call().json(), ] )
tests/test_openlibrary.py:116: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <MagicMock name='get' id='4357540368'> calls = [call('https://openlibrary.org/api/books.json?bibkeys=ISBN:0374202915'), call().raise_for_status(), call().json(), call('https://openlibrary.org/books/OL23575801M.json'), call().raise_for_status(), call().json()] any_order = False
def assert_has_calls(self, calls, any_order=False): """assert the mock has been called with the specified calls. The `mock_calls` list is checked for the calls.
If `any_order` is False (the default) then the calls must be sequential. There can be extra calls before or after the specified calls.
If `any_order` is True then the calls can be in any order, but they must all appear in `mock_calls`.""" expected = [self._call_matcher(c) for c in calls] cause = next((e for e in expected if isinstance(e, Exception)), None) all_calls = _CallList(self._call_matcher(c) for c in self.mock_calls) if not any_order: if expected not in all_calls: if cause is None: problem = 'Calls not found.' else: problem = ('Error processing expected calls.\n' 'Errors: {}').format( [e if isinstance(e, Exception) else None for e in expected]) > raise AssertionError( f'{problem}\n' f'Expected: {_CallList(calls)}' f'{self._calls_repr(prefix="Actual").rstrip(".")}' ) from cause E AssertionError: Calls not found. E Expected: [call('https://openlibrary.org/api/books.json?bibkeys=ISBN:0374202915'), E call().raise_for_status(), E call().json(), E call('https://openlibrary.org/books/OL23575801M.json'), E call().raise_for_status(), E call().json()] E Actual: [call('https://openlibrary.org/api/books.json?bibkeys=ISBN:0374202915'), E call().json(), E call('https://openlibrary.org/books/OL23575801M.json'), E call().json()]
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/unittest/mock.py:976: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_get_notfound
test_load_by_isbn

failure.message: AssertionError: Calls not found. Expected: [call('https://openlibrary.org/api/books.json?bibkeys=ISBN:0137903952'), call().raise_for_status(), call().json(), call('https://openlibrary.org/books/OL3702561M.json'), call().raise_for_status(), call().json(), call('https://openlibrary.org/authors/OL440500A.json'), call().raise_for_status(), call().json()] Actual: [call('https://openlibrary.org/api/books.json?bibkeys=ISBN:0137903952'), call().json(), call('https://openlibrary.org/books/OL3702561M.json'), call().json(), call('https://openlibrary.org/authors/OL440500A.json'), call().json()]

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_openlibrary.TestFullEditionGet testMethod=test_load_by_isbn> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4357408912'>
def test_load_by_isbn(self, mock_get): isbn_key = 'ISBN:0137903952' isbn_bibkeys = { isbn_key: { 'info_url': "https://openlibrary.org/books/%s/Artificial_intelligence" % self.target_olid } } mock_get.return_value.json.side_effect = [ isbn_bibkeys, self.raw_edition.copy(), self.raw_author.copy(), ]
actual = json.loads(jsonpickle.encode(self.ol.Edition.get(isbn='0137903952'))) > mock_get.assert_has_calls( [ call(f"{self.ol.base_url}/api/books.json?bibkeys={isbn_key}"), call().raise_for_status(), call().json(), call(f"{self.ol.base_url}/books/{self.target_olid}.json"), call().raise_for_status(), call().json(), call(f"{self.ol.base_url}/authors/OL440500A.json"), call().raise_for_status(), call().json(), ] )
tests/test_openlibrary.py:340: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <MagicMock name='get' id='4357408912'> calls = [call('https://openlibrary.org/api/books.json?bibkeys=ISBN:0137903952'), call().raise_for_status(), call().json(), call('https://openlibrary.org/books/OL3702561M.json'), call().raise_for_status(), call().json(), ...] any_order = False
def assert_has_calls(self, calls, any_order=False): """assert the mock has been called with the specified calls. The `mock_calls` list is checked for the calls.
If `any_order` is False (the default) then the calls must be sequential. There can be extra calls before or after the specified calls.
If `any_order` is True then the calls can be in any order, but they must all appear in `mock_calls`.""" expected = [self._call_matcher(c) for c in calls] cause = next((e for e in expected if isinstance(e, Exception)), None) all_calls = _CallList(self._call_matcher(c) for c in self.mock_calls) if not any_order: if expected not in all_calls: if cause is None: problem = 'Calls not found.' else: problem = ('Error processing expected calls.\n' 'Errors: {}').format( [e if isinstance(e, Exception) else None for e in expected]) > raise AssertionError( f'{problem}\n' f'Expected: {_CallList(calls)}' f'{self._calls_repr(prefix="Actual").rstrip(".")}' ) from cause E AssertionError: Calls not found. E Expected: [call('https://openlibrary.org/api/books.json?bibkeys=ISBN:0137903952'), E call().raise_for_status(), E call().json(), E call('https://openlibrary.org/books/OL3702561M.json'), E call().raise_for_status(), E call().json(), E call('https://openlibrary.org/authors/OL440500A.json'), E call().raise_for_status(), E call().json()] E Actual: [call('https://openlibrary.org/api/books.json?bibkeys=ISBN:0137903952'), E call().json(), E call('https://openlibrary.org/books/OL3702561M.json'), E call().json(), E call('https://openlibrary.org/authors/OL440500A.json'), E call().json()]

testcase.name: test_load_by_olid

failure.message: AssertionError: Calls not found. Expected: [call('https://openlibrary.org/books/OL3702561M.json'), call().raise_for_status(), call().json(), call('https://openlibrary.org/authors/OL440500A.json'), call().raise_for_status(), call().json()] Actual: [call('https://openlibrary.org/books/OL3702561M.json'), call().json(), call('https://openlibrary.org/authors/OL440500A.json'), call().json()]

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_openlibrary.TestFullEditionGet testMethod=test_load_by_olid> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4357054288'>
def test_load_by_olid(self, mock_get): mock_get.return_value.json.side_effect = [ self.raw_edition.copy(), self.raw_author.copy(), ]
actual = json.loads( jsonpickle.encode(self.ol.Edition.get(olid=self.target_olid)) ) > mock_get.assert_has_calls( [ call(f"{self.ol.base_url}/books/{self.target_olid}.json"), call().raise_for_status(), call().json(), call(f"{self.ol.base_url}/authors/OL440500A.json"), call().raise_for_status(), call().json(), ] )

self = <MagicMock name='get' id='4357054288'> calls = [call('https://openlibrary.org/books/OL3702561M.json'), call().raise_for_status(), call().json(), call('https://openlibrary.org/authors/OL440500A.json'), call().raise_for_status(), call().json()] any_order = False
def assert_has_calls(self, calls, any_order=False): """assert the mock has been called with the specified calls. The `mock_calls` list is checked for the calls.
If `any_order` is False (the default) then the calls must be sequential. There can be extra calls before or after the specified calls.
If `any_order` is True then the calls can be in any order, but they must all appear in `mock_calls`.""" expected = [self._call_matcher(c) for c in calls] cause = next((e for e in expected if isinstance(e, Exception)), None) all_calls = _CallList(self._call_matcher(c) for c in self.mock_calls) if not any_order: if expected not in all_calls: if cause is None: problem = 'Calls not found.' else: problem = ('Error processing expected calls.\n' 'Errors: {}').format( [e if isinstance(e, Exception) else None for e in expected]) > raise AssertionError( f'{problem}\n' f'Expected: {_CallList(calls)}' f'{self._calls_repr(prefix="Actual").rstrip(".")}' ) from cause E AssertionError: Calls not found. E Expected: [call('https://openlibrary.org/books/OL3702561M.json'), E call().raise_for_status(), E call().json(), E call('https://openlibrary.org/authors/OL440500A.json'), E call().raise_for_status(), E call().json()] E Actual: [call('https://openlibrary.org/books/OL3702561M.json'), E call().json(), E call('https://openlibrary.org/authors/OL440500A.json'), E call().json()]
